philosophy: STRICT DIÁTAXIS. Every page belongs to exactly ONE of four non-overlapping modes, distinguished by the reader's posture, not the topic: Tutorials (learning by doing — a guided arc, holds your hand, optimizes for confidence), How-to (solving by doing — task recipes for someone who already knows the basics, optimizes for getting unstuck), Reference (looking up — dry, exhaustive, source-derived, optimizes for trust/correctness), Explanation (understanding by reading — the "why" and the design, no step-by-step, optimizes for mental models). A topic like "branching" therefore appears once per mode at most — once as a tutorial step, once as a how-to recipe, once as an Explanation of the branch/merge model, once in the keyword Reference — and these four are explicitly cross-linked, never merged. The single hardest rule we adopt from Diátaxis purists: Tutorial pages may NOT answer "how do I do X in production" and How-to pages may NOT teach concepts or wander into "why"; when a page is tempted to do both, it splits. This directly dissolves the audit's central structural defect (3-4 competing front doors teaching the same foundations 3-4x): each front door is reassigned to exactly one quadrant, so "what is a pipeline" has exactly one Explanation home and one Tutorial appearance, never four. Borrowed patterns named explicitly: the four-mode split and the "one page = one mode" discipline are Diátaxis; "tutorial-first, one concept per page, every snippet runnable" is FastAPI; "User-Guide(=Explanation) + API-Reference + executable examples gallery as three distinct estates" is scikit-learn; "modern mkdocs-material-grade clarity, source-derived reference, doctest-on-CI" is Polars/Pydantic; and "one unambiguous Start here" is the universal landing-page convention. Maintainability principle layered on top: single source of truth per concept (Explanation owns concepts; Reference owns parameters; nothing else may restate them — they link).
one_line_pitch: Train, evaluate, and deploy NIRS/spectroscopy models in one inline pipeline: result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[...], dataset=...).
top_level_nav: Start here (landing + 5-min Quickstart — the single front door)
Examples gallery (executable, CI-run scripts, cross-linked from all four modes)
front_door_resolution: Today there are FOUR competing front doors plus a landing page: getting_started/ (install+quickstart+tutorial+concepts), concepts/ (7 conceptual pages), onboarding/ (mental_models/persona_paths/*-workflow — labeled "Architecture Deep Dive" but really a second concepts tree), and ai_onboarding.md (a single-file orientation). Strict Diátaxis forces each into exactly one quadrant, eliminating the competition: (1) index.md becomes the ONLY landing page — a thin router with one unmistakable "Start here -> Quickstart" call-to-action plus four mode cards (Tutorials/How-to/Reference/Explanation) and one Examples card; it teaches nothing itself. (2) getting_started/{installation,quickstart,tutorial} -> TUTORIALS quadrant (this is the canonical learning on-ramp). getting_started/concepts.md is DELETED, its unique content folded into Explanation. (3) concepts/ (all 7 pages) -> EXPLANATION quadrant — this becomes the single canonical conceptual home; it is the winner of the "what is a pipeline/dataset/CV" competition. (4) onboarding/ is DISSOLVED: mental_models.md + the *-workflow pages merge INTO the matching concepts/* Explanation pages (mental_models -> Explanation overview; data_workflow -> concepts/datasets; pipeline_workflow -> concepts/pipelines; workspace_intro -> a new Explanation "workspace & reproducibility" page; controllers_intro -> Contributing/internals). persona_paths.md is DELETED as a page and its value re-expressed as "learning paths" links on the landing page and at the top of the Tutorials index (Diátaxis treats persona routing as navigation, not content — and it carried 5 broken refs + the fake load_dataset(), audit #7/#8). (5) ai_onboarding.md -> kept as a single non-navigation file at a stable URL (llms.txt-style machine on-ramp) but REMOVED from the human toctree so it stops competing as a front door; its 16 inline-style violations and stale 0.8.6/license get fixed, and it is regenerated to point at the canonical Quickstart. Net: exactly one Start-here (landing -> Quickstart), exactly one conceptual home (Explanation/concepts), zero remaining front-door overlap.

section_design.purpose: One unambiguous entry. Router + the single 5-minute path to a first successful run(). Teaches nothing beyond the one Quickstart snippet.
section_design.contains: Rewritten index.md (thin router, four mode cards + Examples card, ONE 'Start here' CTA); getting_started/quickstart.md. Landing also hosts the small 'learning paths' link block that replaces persona_paths routing. Metadata (version/license/citation) sourced from nirs4all.__version__ + LICENSE, fixing audit #1/#2/#16.

section_design.purpose: Guided, confidence-building arcs a newcomer follows top-to-bottom; every step runs; no 'why', no production edge-cases (those link out to Explanation/How-to).
section_design.contains: getting_started/installation.md, quickstart.md, tutorial.md (the progressive hello-world->stacking arc). New 'first classification' and 'try-without-data via generate()' mini-tutorials drawn from examples/user/01_getting_started/U01-U04 and U05_synthetic. Each tutorial step cross-links to its Explanation concept and its gallery script.

section_design.purpose: Recipes for users who already ran a pipeline: 'how do I do X'. Goal-titled, self-contained, no concept-teaching (links to Explanation instead).
section_design.contains: Almost all of today's user_guide/** (the how-to epicenter): preprocessing/*, models/{training,hyperparameter_tuning,deep_learning,tabpfn}, pipelines/{branching,merging,stacking,generators,multi_source,cache_optimization}, data/{loading_data,sample_filtering,signal_types}, augmentation/*, deployment/{export_bundles,prediction_model_reuse,retrain_transfer}, predictions/{making_predictions,exporting_models,analyzing_results}, visualization/{shap,prediction_charts,in_pipeline_charts,pipeline_diagram}, troubleshooting/{faq,dataset_troubleshooting} (rewritten to fix y_column #11, bundle= #4, SklearnWrapper #12, phantom charts #9/#10), logging.md, migration/* recipes. Conceptual sub-pages currently embedded here (e.g. preprocessing/overview, data/index 'concepts') move OUT to Explanation.

section_design.purpose: Dry, exhaustive, source-derived lookup. Single home for every signature, keyword, operator, metric, schema. No tutorials, no prose 'why'.
section_design.contains: All of reference/** (pipeline_syntax, pipeline_keywords, generator_keywords, combination_generator, operator_catalog, transforms, augmentations, models, splitters, filters, configuration/*, metrics, predictions_api, cli, workspace, storage) PLUS the unified autodoc API home (today's api/ tree). The hand-written api/{module_api,sklearn_integration,storage,workspace}.md and reference/api/session.md collapse into the reference/ equivalents (audit #18, #26). run()/predict() param lists completed (audit #22/#23/#24).

section_design.purpose: The single canonical 'why/what' home — concepts and design, read not executed. Resolves the 3-4x concept duplication.
section_design.contains: concepts/{pipelines,datasets,cross_validation,branching_and_merging,generators,augmentation,predictions_and_deployment} as the conceptual core; ABSORBS onboarding/{mental_models,data_workflow,pipeline_workflow,workspace_intro}; absorbs scoring_and_refit.md and the conceptual halves of preprocessing/overview, data/index, understanding_predictions, signal_types. Plus a new 'workspace & reproducibility' Explanation page (from workspace_intro + storage rationale, NOT the storage schema which stays in Reference).

section_design.purpose: scikit-learn-style runnable gallery: the ~80 examples/*.py rendered as a browsable, CI-executed gallery, the proof-by-execution layer cross-linked from every mode. Not a fifth mode — a cross-cutting evidence estate.
section_design.contains: examples/user/** and examples/developer/** rendered (gallery, not duplicated prose). Replaces the 8 hand-written docs/source/examples/user/*.md wrapper pages (which carried stale counts #16, undocumented U05/U06 #17, SklearnWrapper #12) with auto-generated-from-source gallery pages. Count derived programmatically.

section_design.purpose: scikit-learn's 'Developer's Guide' estate: a separate audience (contributors), kept out of the user-facing four modes so it never competes as a front door.
section_design.contains: developer/{architecture,pipeline_architecture,controllers,caching,artifacts,artifacts_internals,metadata,outputs_vs_artifacts,testing} + onboarding/controllers_intro + developer/synthetic. Internally split by the SAME Diátaxis modes (how-to: 'write a custom controller'; reference: controller registry; explanation: architecture). Roadmap/Phase leakage (audit #33) moved to _internal.

examples_placement: Examples become a first-class EXECUTABLE GALLERY (scikit-learn pattern), not a fifth Diátaxis mode and not embedded prose. examples/*.py stay the source of truth and are rendered into gallery pages auto-generated FROM the scripts (so the 8 hand-written docs/source/examples/user/*.md wrappers — which drifted: stale counts, undocumented U05/U06, SklearnWrapper — are deleted and regenerated). The gallery is a cross-cutting estate referenced FROM all four modes: a Tutorial step links to its gallery script as 'the full runnable version', a How-to recipe links to the script that demonstrates it, Reference pages link to a canonical example per operator. On the U/D/R taxonomy: U-prefixed (user) examples are surfaced as the visible gallery (mapped onto the four modes by section: 01_getting_started feeds Tutorials, the rest feed How-to); D-prefixed (developer) examples live under the Contributing & internals gallery; R-prefixed (reference) examples are wired to Reference pages as canonical-syntax proofs and are NOT a separate user-facing gallery section. The missing R04 (audit #34) is resolved by converting the stale, un-CI'd examples/predictions.ipynb (health 1/5) into a runnable examples/reference/R04_visualization.py using the public PredictionAnalyzer API, filling the R04 gap and killing the broken notebook in one move. Example INVENTORY is derived programmatically from disk into one manifest consumed by run.sh, run_ci_examples.sh, README, and the gallery generator, so the count is stamped once everywhere (audit #16/#34). Weekly-only CI is replaced (see safety_net): a fast U-subset runs per-PR so a gallery script can't sit broken on main for 7 days.
reference_strategy: ONE Reference home. Today the same API surface is documented twice: the api/ tree (4 hand-written .md + 357 autodoc stubs, carrying 'Version 5.0', legacy runs/ framing, ~66-100% duplication with reference/) and the reference/ tree. Unification: (1) reference/ is the single canonical Reference estate and the only thing in the top-level Reference nav. (2) The 357 autodoc stubs are kept (they are source-derived and cheap to trust) but relocated UNDER reference/ as 'reference/api/' (the generated API index), reachable from the Reference landing — not a parallel top-level 'api/'. (3) The 4 hand-written api/*.md pages are MERGED into their reference/ counterparts and then deleted: api/module_api -> folded into reference public-API pages (predict() first-param fixed source->model, audit #22); api/sklearn_integration -> reference/ sklearn page; api/storage -> reference/storage (drop the ~800-LOC duplicate, drop 'Version 5.0', drop runs/ framing #18); api/workspace -> reference/workspace (drop the 100%-duplicate PipelineLibrary section). reference/api/session.md is wired into the Reference toctree (fixes orphan #26) or merged into the session reference. (4) Generator syntax, today split across generator_keywords/combination_generator/pipeline_syntax with drift (#38), is consolidated to ONE canonical generator-keywords reference page that the others link to. (5) The narrative pipeline_syntax.md keeps the prose-syntax role but every parameter table is generated/validated against source, and its 'Dec 2025 / Phase 3' footer is removed (#2). Single source of truth: Reference owns parameters and schemas; Explanation links to Reference for exactness and never restates a signature.
style_guide_stance: The inline-pipeline mandate ('1 pipeline written INLINE inside run(pipeline=[...], dataset=...), no external variable') is presented as the ONE house style, stated once in a short CONTRIBUTING 'Documentation style' section and shown by example on the very first Quickstart, then enforced mechanically — never re-explained per page. Enforcement (turning aspiration into infrastructure, audit recommendation #2): a CI lint (ruff custom rule or a dedicated grep gate in scripts/) over docs/source/**.md fenced python blocks AND examples/**.py that flags the anti-pattern 'pipeline = [...]' followed by run(pipeline, ...). The 648 violations across 140 files (audit §3) are cleared by one mechanical sweep, then the gate prevents regrowth (without it the audit shows 648 -> 700 next release). Documented exceptions are explicit and machine-readable (a noqa-style marker / allowlist) so they don't reopen the loophole: (a) deliberate reuse/comparison examples that feed the SAME pipeline through several run()/PipelineRunner/Session calls (a few in 06_deployment / 06_internals); (b) synthesis/utility scripts that are not pipeline tutorials (developer/02_generators/D05-D09, examples/_internal/*); and (c) compliant inline calls merely split across multiple physical lines (which the naive grep false-positives on, per Codex's 605-vs-655 note). The gate matches the variable-assignment-then-pass pattern specifically, not 'the word pipeline on its own line', to avoid those false positives. Stance summary: mandate = enforced by CI, taught once, exceptions allow-listed and re-examined (most exception scripts can still be inlined).
safety_net: Four layers, because the root cause the audit found is 'nothing that executes documentation exists in the safety net' (every wrong-API defect survived because nothing ran it). (1) Doc-snippet execution: enable myst_nb / doctest on docs/source so fenced python blocks (or marked ones) execute in CI — this is what would have caught result.predict() (#3), bundle= (#4), plot_predictions() (#9), SklearnWrapper (#12), Predictions.load() (#14). Mirrors Polars/Pydantic doctest-on-CI. (2) Per-PR examples: move examples.yml off its weekly cron — run a fast U-subset (01_getting_started + one per section) on every push/PR, full ~80-script suite stays nightly/weekly. No gallery script sits broken on main for up to 7 days (#19, #32, #34). (3) Tighten nitpick: remove the blanket nitpick_ignore_regex that masks nirs4all.{data,pipeline,api}.* xrefs, and reconcile the CI-vs-RTD strictness disagreement (docs.yml uses -W --keep-going while .readthedocs.yaml sets fail_on_warning:false) so a typo'd :class: ref FAILS the build instead of shipping (#18, §5.3A). (4) Metadata-from-source + CI grep gates: version/license/copyright/citation derived from nirs4all.__version__ and LICENSE (not hand-typed), with a CI grep that fails if the landing/citation license string diverges from the LICENSE dual-license statement (default AGPL-3.0-or-later; CeCILL is a legit optional variant so the gate checks the STRING matches, it does NOT ban the CeCILL token — audit #1) and if any version string != __version__ (#2); plus the inline-style grep gate (above) and the programmatic example-count stamp (#16). Net effect: the entire 'ships silently' class of defects becomes 'fails the build'.
pros: Eliminates the central structural defect cleanly: each of the 4 competing front doors maps to exactly one quadrant, so every foundational concept has exactly one canonical home (no more 3-4x duplication, no ambiguous 'where do I start').

Single source of truth per concept is structurally enforced by the modes: Explanation owns 'why', Reference owns parameters, neither restates the other — drift between them becomes impossible by construction, which is the maintainability win.
Newcomer reaches first run() fast: landing -> Quickstart is a single unambiguous click-path with one runnable snippet, the FastAPI tutorial-first pattern.

The safety-net + style gate convert the audit's entire 'ships silently' defect class (wrong-API copy-paste, 648 style violations, metadata drift, broken xrefs) into build failures, so the rewrite STAYS correct.
Reference is unified to one home (no api/ vs reference/ split, no Version-5.0 duplicates), and the 357 autodoc stubs are retained source-derived under it — high trust, low maintenance.
Examples become an executable, CI-run, programmatically-counted gallery cross-linked from all modes — proof-by-execution rather than drifting prose wrappers.
Maps onto a well-understood, documented theory (Diátaxis) so future contributors know exactly which quadrant a new page belongs in — durable governance, not a one-off cleanup.
cons: Strict Diátaxis splits topics across four pages (a 'branching' learner must visit Tutorial + How-to + Explanation + Reference); without disciplined cross-linking this can feel fragmented vs a single all-in-one branching page.

High migration cost: nearly every one of the 119 narrative pages must be re-homed, several merged (onboarding dissolved into concepts) and a few deleted (getting_started/concepts, persona_paths) — a large, careful page-by-page move, not a tweak.

The 'one page = one mode' purity is hard to police by humans; pages drift back toward mixing concept+recipe unless reviewers enforce it (needs a contributor checklist, partially mitigated by the CI gates which only cover style/metadata/refs, not mode-purity).

Dissolving onboarding/persona_paths removes an explicit role-based on-ramp that some users liked; replacing it with 'learning paths' links is lighter and risks under-serving the operator-author persona if not curated well.

Doc-snippet execution + per-PR examples lengthen CI and require fixtures/sample-data so snippets actually run headless (plots_visible=False, tiny synthetic datasets) — real engineering setup before the gate can be turned on.
ai_onboarding.md kept as a non-nav machine file is a small ongoing maintenance item (must be regenerated when the API changes) that sits slightly outside the four-mode model.
newcomer_path: Land on index.md (the only front door) -> single prominent 'Start here -> Quickstart' button (the four mode cards and Examples card are secondary) -> Quickstart page: pip install nirs4all, then ONE runnable inline snippet 'result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(n_splits=3), {\"model\": PLSRegression(n_components=10)}], dataset=...)' with a 'no data? use nirs4all.generate.regression(...)' one-liner so they succeed with zero files -> they run it, see result.best_rmse / result.best_r2 and result.export('exports/best_model.n4a') (first successful run() achieved in ~5 min, every line copy-paste-correct because the snippet is doctest-executed in CI) -> the Quickstart's single 'Next' link sends them to the Tutorial (progressive hello-world -> preprocessing -> CV -> stacking) for their SECOND task, with each tutorial step cross-linking sideways to its Explanation concept ('why SNV?'), its How-to recipe ('how to export a bundle'), and its runnable gallery script. No competing 'getting started vs onboarding vs concepts' fork ever appears on this path.

one_line_pitch: nirs4all: from raw spectra to a trained, exportable NIRS model in one inline run() call; follow the Tutorial top to bottom and you never hit a dead end.
philosophy: TUTORIAL-FIRST, FastAPI-model. The spine is ONE long, strictly progressive guided tutorial where each page adds exactly one concept, every block is a complete runnable script, and every pipeline is inline inside run(pipeline=[...], dataset=...). How-to recipes, the example gallery, the API reference, explanation notes, and contributor internals are APPENDICES to that spine, reached when the reader has a task rather than a learning goal. I borrow Diataxis's four MODES but deliberately privilege the Tutorial axis instead of giving the four equal top-level weight (the FastAPI move). scikit-learn supplies the executable-gallery pattern (runnable .py harvested into a gallery, never a parallel doc set). Polars/Pydantic supply single-concept-per-page clarity and one unambiguous Start Here. Invariants: one canonical learning path, one source of truth per concept, every shipped snippet executed in CI, metadata derived from source. Time-to-first-successful-run() is the metric the IA optimizes.
top_level_nav: Get Started (install + 60-second first run + Try Without Data)
Tutorial (the progressive spine, Lesson 1..N, strictly linear)
front_door_resolution: Exactly ONE front door: a Get Started page (only thing above the fold) whose job is install -> one copy-paste inline run() on synthetic data (generate.regression()) -> a single 'Continue to the Tutorial' button. Collapse: (1) getting_started/ PROMOTED to canonical entry (installation.md -> Get Started/install; quickstart.md -> first-run; tutorial.md -> Tutorial Lesson 1-2; getting_started/concepts.md merged into the Tutorial body, residual why-prose to Concepts). (2) concepts/ DEMOTED from front door to the thin Concepts/Explanation appendix; each concepts/*.md becomes the 'Why it works' note for its lesson, never a competing path. (3) onboarding/ SPLIT: user-facing mental-models/persona prose folds into the Tutorial intro and Concepts; controllers_intro/workspace_intro/data_workflow/pipeline_workflow move to Contributing & Internals; directory deleted as a front door. (4) ai_onboarding.md REMOVED from human nav, reduced to one machine-readable /llms.txt at the doc root, metadata-from-source (fixing stale 0.8.6/CeCILL). (5) developer/index.md becomes Contributing & Internals, not a top-level door. Net: 5 doors -> 1; the six-card landing grid becomes a single Start Here card plus a small 'I already know what I want' link to Recipes/Reference/Gallery.

section_design.purpose: The one on-ramp. Sub-5-minute time-to-first-success. No concepts, no choices: install, run, succeed, hand off.
section_design.contains: installation.md + quickstart.md merged and deduped against the install block duplicated in index.md; the 'Try Without Data' generate.regression() snippet as the zero-dataset path; ends with one 'Continue to the Tutorial' link.

section_design.purpose: Strictly-linear course. Each lesson adds exactly ONE concept; every snippet a runnable inline-run(); persistent prev/next rail.
section_design.contains: tutorial.md -> Lesson 1-2, then one lesson per concept from the strongest page: Pipelines (concepts/pipelines + writing_pipelines), Datasets/loading (concepts/datasets + data/loading_data), Preprocessing (preprocessing/overview), Cross-validation (concepts/cross_validation), Models/scoring (models/training + scoring_and_refit), Results/predictions (predictions/understanding_predictions + making_predictions), Visualization (visualization/index corrected), Tuning (models/hyperparameter_tuning), Branching/merging (concepts/branching_and_merging), Generators (concepts/generators), Augmentation (concepts/augmentation), Export/deploy (concepts/predictions_and_deployment + deployment/index), Retrain/transfer (deployment/retrain_transfer). Each ends 'Run this yourself ->' (Gallery) + 'Why it works ->' (Concepts).

section_design.purpose: Task-indexed, goal-in-the-title answers for one specific job. Each self-contained, still inline run().
section_design.contains: user_guide slices: deployment/{export_bundles,prediction_model_reuse}, models/{deep_learning,tabpfn_nirs}, pipelines/{stacking,multi_source,cache_optimization}, data/{sample_filtering,signal_types,aggregation}, augmentation/sample_augmentation_guide, predictions/{advanced_predictions,exporting_models}, visualization/{in_pipeline_charts,shap,pipeline_diagram}, logging, preprocessing/{snv,resampler,handbook,cheatsheet}, troubleshooting/{faq (target_column in global_params),dataset_troubleshooting}.

section_design.purpose: scikit-learn executable gallery: runnable examples/*.py ARE the gallery, auto-rendered, no drifting parallel prose.
section_design.contains: examples/user/** (U), developer/** (D), reference/** (R) harvested via sphinx-gallery/literalinclude with header docstrings as captions; the eight docs/source/examples/*.md hand-written pages deleted and replaced by generated index pages, killing U05/U06-undocumented and stale-count drift.

section_design.purpose: Single lookup home: stable-contract API + pipeline syntax + operator catalog, terse and complete, generated where possible.
section_design.contains: reference/** canonical: pipeline_syntax, pipeline_keywords, generator_keywords/combination_generator deduped to one, operator_catalog + transforms/models/splitters/filters/augmentations/metrics, storage, workspace, cli, configuration/*, predictions_api. api/{module_api,sklearn_integration,storage,workspace}.md merged in and deleted; 357 api/*.rst autodoc stubs mounted under reference/api/ beneath curated pages; reference/api/session.md wired into reference/index.

section_design.purpose: Thin why layer, NOT a learning path; reached from a lesson's 'Why it works'. One concept per page, no competing on-ramp.
section_design.contains: explanation residue of concepts/** after teaching moved to the Tutorial (why SNV, why OOF CV, why inline pipelines, leakage safety, workspace model) + onboarding/mental_models.md; rationale/diagrams only, linking down to Recipes/Reference, up to the lesson.

section_design.purpose: Maintainer/extension audience, walled off so it never competes for the newcomer's attention.
section_design.contains: developer/** (architecture, pipeline_architecture, controllers, artifacts, artifacts_internals, caching, metadata, outputs_vs_artifacts, synthetic, testing) + onboarding/{controllers_intro,workspace_intro,data_workflow,pipeline_workflow} + migration/{duckdb_to_sqlite,storage_migration_webapp} + troubleshooting/migration; Phase/roadmap leakage scrubbed to docs/_internal.

section_design.purpose: Project metadata, all source-derived so it cannot drift.
section_design.contains: changelog, license (dual AGPL-3.0-or-later default), citation (version from nirs4all.__version__), roadmap pointer; the wrong license/version/copyright strings in index.md/ai_onboarding/conf.py sourced here once.

examples_placement: Examples are a first-class AUTO-HARVESTED gallery (scikit-learn model), never a hand-maintained parallel set. The runnable examples/*.py are the single source of truth; the eight docs/source/examples/*.md narrative pages are deleted and regenerated as gallery index pages via sphinx-gallery/literalinclude, so the U05/U06-undocumented gap (#17) and the 50+/60+/66/67 count drift (#16) disappear structurally; counts become a derived number stamped once. The U/D/R taxonomy is surfaced as three gallery TRACKS mapping onto the audience split: U examples are linked inline from the matching Tutorial lesson via 'Run this yourself ->' (each lesson points at its U-file), so the gallery doubles as the tutorial's runnable backing; D examples sit under an 'Advanced/Internals' track cross-linked from Contributing & Internals; R examples back Reference pages (R01 under pipeline_syntax, R02/R03 under generator/keyword reference). Missing R04 is resolved by converting the stale 1.8MB examples/predictions.ipynb into runnable examples/reference/R04_visualization.py using the real PredictionAnalyzer API, filling the hole AND removing the un-CI'd broken notebook in one move; the notebook is dropped. Weekly-only CI is replaced by per-PR execution of a fast U-track subset plus the full R-track (full U+D+R stays on the weekly cron), so every example backing a lesson or Reference page is proven runnable on the PR that touches it.
reference_strategy: ONE reference home: reference/. The api/ tree dissolves INTO it, not beside it. The four hand-written api/*.md merge into their reference/ twins and are deleted: api/storage.md -> reference/storage.md, api/workspace.md -> reference/workspace.md (killing the 66-100% duplication and the Version-5.0/legacy-runs framing, #18); api/module_api.md and api/sklearn_integration.md fold into reference/predictions_api.md and a reference/sklearn page (fixing predict() first-param 'source'->'model', #22). The 357 api/*.rst autodoc stubs are kept but mounted UNDER reference as the generated member-level layer (reference/api/) beneath curated pages: a reader lands on a curated page (e.g. reference/predictions_api with the run/predict/explain contract and the 4 missing params refit/cache/project/report_naming, #23) and drills into autodoc only for exhaustive member listings. Curated pages own narrative and signatures; autodoc is the exhaustive index. Docstrings feeding autodoc are corrected at source (Predictions.load() docstring in visualization/predictions.py:76, #14) so the generated layer stops inheriting stale calls. reference/api/session.md is wired into reference/index (no longer orphaned, #26). Generator syntax, triplicated across generator_keywords/combination_generator/pipeline_syntax (#38), collapses to one canonical generator reference page; the others cross-link to it.
style_guide_stance: The inline-pipeline mandate is THE house style and the most visible expression of the library pitch: taught explicitly in Get Started ('the pipeline is written inline, in one shot, inside run(), no external variable; that is the nirs4all way') and then modeled, never violated, in every Tutorial lesson, Recipe, and Gallery example. It is enforced infrastructure, not aspiration. A CI lint (ruff custom rule or dedicated grep gate over docs/source/**.md and examples/**.py) fails the build on the anti-pattern pipeline = [ ... ] then run(pipeline, ...), stopping 648 from regrowing to 700. The lint ships with exactly TWO documented allowlisted exceptions so it is precise not blunt: (1) deliberate reuse/comparison scripts running the SAME pipeline through several run()/PipelineRunner/Session calls (a handful in 06_deployment/06_internals); (2) synthesis/utility scripts that are not pipeline tutorials (developer/02_generators/D05-D09, examples/_internal/*). Those exceptions live in a single STYLE.md that is also the allowlist the lint reads, so the carve-out is auditable and cannot silently widen. Compliant multi-line inline calls are recognized (not flagged). The 648 existing sites are cleared by one mechanical sweep BEFORE the gate turns on, so it starts green.
safety_net: Documentation enters CI as executable, replacing the 'nothing runs the docs' root cause. Five gates. (1) DOC-SNIPPET EXECUTION: every runnable block in docs/source/**.md is run via myst-nb/sphinx doctest, or via literalinclude-from-tested-examples so snippets are slices of already-tested .py; turns result.predict()/bundle=/plot_predictions()/SklearnWrapper drift from ships-silently into fails-the-build. (2) PER-PR EXAMPLES: examples.yml moves off the weekly Sunday cron to per-PR execution of a fast U-track + R-track subset (full U+D+R stays weekly); the two never-run demos (D01_metadata_branching, D03_repetition_transform) get real run() calls or are relabeled syntax-only. (3) NITPICK TIGHTENING: conf.py blanket nitpick_ignore_regex over nirs4all.{data,pipeline,api}.* and the myst.xref_missing/ref.* suppressions removed so typo'd :class: xrefs fail; CI (-W --keep-going) and .readthedocs.yaml (fail_on_warning) reconciled to the SAME strictness. (4) METADATA-FROM-SOURCE: conf.py release read from nirs4all.__version__, copyright bumped, citation/license strings on landing+About+llms.txt generated from LICENSE and __version__; a CI grep gate asserts the rendered license matches the LICENSE dual-license statement (AGPL-3.0-or-later default) WITHOUT banning the legitimate CeCILL token. (5) CI GREP GATES: the inline-style lint, an example-count gate (stamped number must equal harvested file count), and a broken-example-ref gate (every examples/*.py path referenced in docs must exist on disk, catching persona_paths/U21-U22/export_bundles dead refs). Together these convert the entire wrong-API defect class to build-failures and keep the rewrite correct over time.
pros: Single unambiguous on-ramp: 5 front doors collapse to one Get Started -> Tutorial spine, eliminating the central structural defect (#6) where concepts were taught 3-4x with no canonical source.
Ruthless time-to-first-success: a newcomer copies one inline run() on synthetic data and succeeds before any concept is required (the FastAPI 'works on line 1' effect).
One source of truth per concept: TAUGHT in one Tutorial lesson, EXPLAINED in one Concepts page, LOOKED UP in one Reference page; no fourth copy.
Examples stop drifting: the gallery is generated from the runnable .py, so counts and U05/U06 coverage can never disagree with reality.
The style mandate becomes self-reinforcing: taught first, lint-enforced, so docs model the house style at scale instead of teaching the anti-pattern 648 times.
Reference de-duplication: api/ dissolves into reference/, removing double-documentation and the Version-5.0/legacy-runs contradictions.
Maintainability: every shipped snippet runs per-PR and all metadata is source-derived, so the wrong-API class (#3-14) becomes structurally impossible to reintroduce silently.
AI/agent consumers get one clean metadata-correct llms.txt instead of a stale ai_onboarding page competing in the nav.
cons: A strictly-linear spine serves the linear newcomer best; the expert wanting 'how do I stack models' depends on Recipes/Reference being well-indexed; under-loved appendices hurt the non-linear reader (mitigated by task-titled Recipes and per-lesson cross-links).
Maintaining ONE coherent progressive tutorial demands higher editorial discipline than a loose how-to set: every lesson must hold 'exactly one new concept', so inserting/reordering is costlier.
Demoting concepts/ and onboarding/ from front doors to appendices is a large one-time migration with real risk of dropping good prose if rushed.

The doc-snippet execution gate adds CI time and a flakiness surface (DL backends, GPU-only TabPFN/NICON); the fast-subset-per-PR design is a compromise, so some D-track breakage still only surfaces weekly.
Auto-harvested gallery + autodoc-under-reference requires sphinx-gallery/myst-nb tooling and a working executable-doc build, more machinery than the current suppress-and-ship setup.
Privileging the tutorial axis is an opinionated bet against equal-weight Diataxis; teams expecting a four-quadrant nav may find Reference/Explanation deliberately understated.
newcomer_path: Land on the docs home -> a single 'Start Here' card (no six-way grid). Click it -> Get Started: run pip install nirs4all, then copy ONE block calling nirs4all.run(pipeline=[MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(n_splits=3), {model: PLSRegression(n_components=10)}], dataset=nirs4all.generate.regression(n_samples=500)); no dataset needed, it prints Best RMSE. That is the FIRST successful run(), reached in under five minutes with zero prior concepts. The page's only forward link is 'Continue to the Tutorial ->'. Click it -> Tutorial Lesson 1 swaps the synthetic dataset for the reader's own folder path (still one inline run()); Lesson 2 adds preprocessing (SNV) as the single new concept; a persistent prev/next rail carries them linearly. The SECOND task is therefore Lesson 2 itself, reached by 'Next ->', with a 'Run this yourself ->' link to examples/user/01_getting_started/U02_basic_regression.py in the Gallery and a 'Why it works ->' link to the Concepts/preprocessing page. No point on this path offers a competing 'also start here' choice.

philosophy: TASK/PERSONA-FIRST. The site is organized around the seven things people actually open nirs4all to do — Regression, Classification, Preprocessing selection, Cross-validation, Deployment/serving, Transfer/retrain, Explainability — and each is a self-contained track that walks data -> inline pipeline -> run() -> result -> predict() to a finished artifact. Underneath sits ONE thin shared "Concepts" layer (the mental model used by every track, never duplicated) and ONE auto-generated Reference. This borrows Diataxis as a vocabulary, not as the top-level skeleton: the four Diataxis modes still exist, but they are re-projected onto goals. Each Task track IS a Diataxis tutorial+how-to fused for one goal (FastAPI's "tutorial-first, every snippet runnable, one concept per page"); Concepts is the Diataxis Explanation layer (scikit-learn's User Guide, shrunk to fundamentals only); Reference is pure Diataxis Reference, fully generated (scikit-learn API Reference). The guiding rule that kills the audit's structural rot: meet the user at the goal, and keep exactly one canonical home per concept — a task track owns the workflow, Concepts owns the idea, Reference owns the signature, and nothing is taught twice. Polars/Pydantic mkdocs-material clarity (one unambiguous Start Here, copy-buttoned runnable blocks) is the surface finish.
one_line_pitch: Pick your goal, copy one inline run(pipeline=[...], dataset=...), get a model you can predict() and ship — nirs4all docs meet you at the task, not the architecture.
top_level_nav: Start here (install + 60-second first run + 'choose your task' router)
Tasks (the 7 goal tracks: Regression, Classification, Preprocessing selection, Cross-validation, Deployment & serving, Transfer & retrain, Explainability)
Concepts (thin shared layer: pipeline & inline run() model, SpectroDataset, generators, branching/merging, workspace & predictions, the result objects)
Examples (runnable gallery, tagged by task track)
Reference (auto-generated API + syntax/operator catalogs + CLI + metrics)
Contributing & internals (was developer/ + onboarding/ architecture, audience-gated)
front_door_resolution: There is exactly ONE front door: a single `index.md` -> `start-here/` directory. The four competitors collapse as follows. (1) `getting_started/` BECOMES Start here: keep install.md and a rewritten quickstart as the literal '60-second first run' (fixing the `result.predict()` defect #3), and turn its `concepts.md`/`tutorial.md` into the 'choose your task' router that links into the seven Task tracks. (2) `concepts/` (8 strong, health-4 pages) is DEMOTED from a front door to the thin shared Concepts layer linked *beneath* the tasks — it stops competing for first contact and becomes the single canonical explanation home. (3) `onboarding/` is SPLIT by audience: its user-facing prose (mental_models, data_workflow, pipeline_workflow) is merged into Concepts (dedup against concepts/, one survives); `persona_paths.md` is DELETED outright — the seven Task tracks ARE the persona paths, done correctly, so its 5 broken refs + non-existent `load_dataset()` (defects #7, #8) die with it; `controllers_intro.md`/`workspace_intro.md`/architecture prose move to Contributing & internals. (4) `ai_onboarding.md` is REWRITTEN to a single machine-readable `llms.txt`-style page under Start here that just points at canonical anchors (no parallel tutorial), killing its 16 style violations and stale 0.8.6/CeCILL metadata (defects #1, #2). Net: 4 front doors -> 1, and the landing toctree drops from 8 overlapping top-level paths to the 6 above.

section_design.purpose: One unambiguous entry: install, a 60-second copy-paste first run() that succeeds on synthetic data, then route to a task. The only page a newcomer must read.
section_design.contains: getting_started/installation.md (kept); getting_started/quickstart.md rewritten as '60-second first run' on nirs4all.generate.regression so it runs with zero user data and fixes result.predict()->nirs4all.predict(); getting_started/concepts.md+tutorial.md folded into a 'Choose your task' router card-grid; ai_onboarding.md rewritten to a thin llms.txt pointer page.

section_design.purpose: End-to-end: load tabular/spectra -> inline preprocessing+PLS -> run() -> read best_rmse/best_r2 -> predict() on new data.
section_design.contains: examples/user/01_getting_started (U01,U02), user_guide/models/training.md, user_guide/scoring_and_refit.md, the regression slices of user_guide/pipelines/writing_pipelines.md.

section_design.purpose: Same arc for categorical targets: task-type detection, PLSDA, confusion-matrix reading.
section_design.contains: examples/user/01_getting_started/U03_basic_classification.py, classification slices of models/ + visualization (plot_confusion_matrix), the recent task-type-resolution work.

section_design.purpose: The signature NIRS goal: compare SNV/MSC/Detrend/SG via _or_ generators and pick the winner with result.top().
section_design.contains: user_guide/preprocessing/* (overview, handbook, cheatsheet, snv, resampler, transfer_preprocessing_selector_cheatsheet), user_guide/pipelines/generators.md, examples/user/03_preprocessing/* and reference/R02; concepts/augmentation linked, not inlined.

section_design.purpose: Honest validation: choose a splitter (KennardStone/SPXY/group), avoid leakage, read CV vs final scores.
section_design.contains: concepts/cross_validation.md (canonical explanation linked), examples/user/05_cross_validation/* (incl. undocumented U05/U06 -> defect #17), user_guide/data/sample_filtering.md + scoring_and_refit.md.

section_design.purpose: Turn a RunResult into a shippable artifact: export().n4a, predict()/chain_id reuse, sklearn wrapper, workspace ops.
section_design.contains: user_guide/deployment/* (fix bundle=->model= #4, U21/U22 refs #15), user_guide/predictions/{making_predictions,exporting_models,advanced_predictions,session_api}.md, examples/user/06_deployment/*; reference/storage+workspace linked once.

section_design.purpose: Adapt an existing model to a new instrument/dataset via retrain(mode=full|transfer|finetune).
section_design.contains: user_guide/deployment/retrain_transfer.md + prediction_model_reuse.md, examples/developer/04_transfer_learning/* (fix wrong import paths #20), transfer-preprocessing analysis pages.

section_design.purpose: Open the model: explain() -> SHAP values, feature importance, which wavelengths matter.
section_design.contains: user_guide/visualization/shap.md, examples/user/07_explainability/*, the ExplainResult workflow; fixes SklearnWrapper->NIRSPipeline #12 and phantom PredictionAnalyzer methods #9.

section_design.purpose: The single canonical explanation of each idea every task reuses — taught ONCE, linked from everywhere.
section_design.contains: concepts/{pipelines,datasets,branching_and_merging,generators,cross_validation,augmentation,predictions_and_deployment}.md merged with surviving onboarding mental_models/data_workflow/pipeline_workflow; the result-object model (RunResult/PredictResult/ExplainResult) from GROUND_TRUTH.

section_design.purpose: Runnable, CI-executed gallery; every snippet in the prose tracks links to its source example here.
section_design.contains: all examples/user + examples/reference (R01-R07) surfaced via sphinx-gallery/literalinclude; docs/source/examples/* pages become thin gallery indexes tagged by task; example-count derived programmatically (#16).

section_design.purpose: Lookup-only, one home, no hand-maintained API prose that can drift.
section_design.contains: autodoc of nirs4all.{api,data,pipeline,...}; reference/{pipeline_syntax,pipeline_keywords,generator_keywords,operator_catalog,transforms,models,splitters,filters,augmentations,metrics,cli,storage,workspace,configuration}.md; the 4 hand-written api/*.md are deleted/merged (#18).

section_design.purpose: Audience-gated home for maintainers; never competes for the newcomer's attention.
section_design.contains: developer/* (architecture, controllers, caching, artifacts*, pipeline_architecture, testing, metadata), onboarding/{controllers_intro,workspace_intro}, migration/*, with internal Phase/roadmap leakage moved to docs/_internal (#33).

examples_placement: Examples become a first-class, CI-executed GALLERY (scikit-learn pattern) rather than a parallel doc tree. `examples/*.py` stay the single source — they already double as integration tests — and are surfaced into docs via sphinx-gallery (or literalinclude with thumbnails) so the rendered page IS the file that CI runs; no copy. Each gallery item is TAGGED to a Task track (U01-U03->Regression/Classification, U03_preprocessing->Preprocessing selection, U05->Cross-validation, U06->Deployment, U07->Explainability, D04->Transfer), and every prose track ends with 'Run it: <gallery link>'. The eight `docs/source/examples/*.md` pages stop being narrative duplicates and become thin auto-generated gallery indexes; the undocumented U05/U06 (#17) appear automatically because the gallery is generated from disk, not hand-listed. U/D/R taxonomy is RE-PROJECTED, not preserved as nav: U/D/R survive only as on-disk prefixes + a difficulty/audience badge (User=in-track, Developer=advanced section of the relevant track or Contributing, Reference=Reference gallery), so users never navigate by 'U vs D' — they navigate by task. Missing R04 is filled by converting the stale 1.8MB predictions.ipynb into a runnable `examples/reference/R04_visualization.py` on the public PredictionAnalyzer API (audit #14/#34), which simultaneously kills the notebook coverage hole and the inventory gap. Weekly CI is replaced: every PR runs a fast tagged subset (one canonical example per task track, ~7-10 files) so a broken example fails in minutes not 7 days; the full ~80-file suite still runs nightly/weekly. One programmatically-derived inventory (`examples/manifest`) feeds run.sh, run_ci_examples.sh, README, and the gallery (#16, #34).
reference_strategy: ONE reference home: `reference/`. The competing `api/` tree is eliminated. The 357 `api/*.rst` autodoc stubs are regenerated by autosummary INTO `reference/api/` (a single autosummary-recursive root, not 357 hand-touched files), so adding a public symbol updates the reference with zero manual edits. The 4 hand-written `api/*.md` pages are resolved individually: `api/storage.md` and `api/workspace.md` (~66-100% duplicates carrying 'Version 5.0', defect #18) are DELETED and their unique content merged into `reference/storage.md`/`reference/workspace.md`; `api/module_api.md` and `api/sklearn_integration.md` content is folded into the generated API pages + the relevant task track, then deleted. `reference/api/session.md` (orphaned, #26) is wired into `reference/index.md`. The result: hand-written reference prose exists ONLY for things autodoc cannot express well (pipeline syntax, keyword tables, operator catalog, CLI), and those are validated against source (see safety-net). The dual `api/` vs `reference/` documentation of the same surface — the audit's #18/§5.2 consolidation target — is structurally impossible afterward because there is one generated tree and one curated tree, with no overlap.
style_guide_stance: The inline-pipeline mandate is presented as the FIRST thing in a short 'Docs code style' page under Contributing and is restated as a one-line callout at the top of every Task track: every example is written `result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[ ...inline... ], dataset=...)` with NO external `pipeline = [...]` variable. It is enforced as infrastructure, not aspiration (audit §8.2): a `ruff`/grep CI lint over `docs/source/**.md` + `examples/**.py` flags any module-level variable whose name contains `pipeline` assigned a list literal then passed positionally to `run()`. The current 648 violations across 140 files are cleared by one mechanical sweep before the gate turns red-on-regression. Two documented exceptions are codified IN the lint config (allowlist comment `# noqa: inline-pipeline` with a reason) and named in the style page: (1) deliberate reuse/comparison scripts that run the SAME pipeline through several run()/Session calls (a few in 06_deployment/06_internals), and (2) synthesis/utility scripts that are not pipeline tutorials (developer/02_generators D05-D09, examples/_internal/*). Everything else must inline. This is the durable fix that stops 648 regrowing to 700.
safety_net: Five layers, all per-PR (audit §5.3 is the root cause). (1) Doc-snippet execution: enable MyST `myst_nb`/doctest so prose code blocks RUN in the build, or use literalinclude pulling from the executed `examples/` — so `result.predict()`, `bundle=`, `plot_predictions()`, `SklearnWrapper`, `Predictions.load()` would fail the build instead of shipping. (2) Per-PR examples: a fast tagged subset (one canonical example per task track) runs on every push; the full suite stays nightly — replacing the cron-Sunday-only schedule that let broken examples live up to 7 days. (3) Tighten nitpick: remove the blanket `nitpick_ignore_regex` over `nirs4all.{data,pipeline,api}.*` and the `myst.xref_missing`/`ref.*` suppression so a typo'd `:class:` ref fails; align CI (`-W --keep-going`) and `.readthedocs.yaml` (`fail_on_warning: true`) to the same strictness. (4) Metadata-from-source: `conf.py` derives `release` from `nirs4all.__version__`, copyright year and license string from `LICENSE`/README; the citation/version/license appear in exactly one templated place — killing the 0.2.1/0.7.1/0.8.6 drift and the CeCILL-as-sole-license compliance defect. (5) CI grep gates: the inline-pipeline lint (above), a license-string assertion matching the dual-license `LICENSE` statement (NOT a CeCILL-token ban — CeCILL is a valid optional variant), and a programmatic example-count stamped everywhere. Together these convert the entire wrong-API defect class from 'ships silently' to 'fails the build'.
pros: Newcomer reaches first successful run() in one screen: Start here -> 60-second synthetic run, no dataset, no architecture reading required (the FastAPI/Polars 'start here' win).

Single source of truth per concept is structurally enforced: a task owns the workflow, Concepts owns the idea, Reference owns the signature — the audit's 3-4 competing front doors and api/<->reference duplication become impossible to recreate.

Matches how NIRS practitioners actually think ('I have a regression problem', 'I need to pick preprocessing'), not how the code is laid out — higher findability than a Diataxis-literal split for a domain library.
The 648-violation style mandate and the metadata/license/version drift move from prose-promises to CI gates, so they stay fixed across releases.
Reference and Examples are generated from source/disk, so they cannot drift: new public symbols and new example files appear automatically; undocumented U05/U06 and missing R04 self-heal.
persona_paths.md's good intent is realized correctly (the tracks) while its broken refs and fake load_dataset() are deleted, not patched.
cons: Some content is legitimately cross-cutting (a preprocessing step used in Regression AND Classification): tracks must link the canonical Concepts/Reference page, never re-explain — requires authoring discipline and review enforcement or duplication creeps back.

Seven task tracks is more top-level surface than a strict 4-mode Diataxis site; risk of a track feeling thin (e.g. Transfer/retrain) until content matures — mitigated by letting thin tracks be a single rich page initially.

Task-first nav can momentarily disorient a user who thinks in Diataxis terms ('where are the tutorials?') — mitigated by badging each track with its Diataxis mode and keeping Reference/Concepts clearly labeled.

Migrating 119 narrative pages + 357 stubs into the new homes is substantial one-time work and every cross-link must be repointed (audit warns a single grep won't catch all refs); needs a page-by-page keep/merge/rewrite/delete map and a link-check gate.

Generated reference (autosummary-recursive over 357 symbols) needs a tightened nitpick to be trustworthy; turning suppression off will surface a backlog of real broken refs that must be burned down before CI can go strict.
newcomer_path: 1) Land on `index.md` -> single 'Start here' card (no competing Getting Started/Concepts/Onboarding choice to make). 2) Click Start here -> Install (`pip install nirs4all`). 3) Scroll to '60-second first run': copy ONE block — `result = nirs4all.run(pipeline=[MinMaxScaler(), ShuffleSplit(n_splits=3), {\"model\": PLSRegression(n_components=10)}], dataset=nirs4all.generate.regression(n_samples=500))` then `print(result.best_rmse)` — it runs with zero user data because the dataset is synthetic. First successful run() achieved here, on screen one. 4) Hit the 'Choose your task' router (the rewritten concepts/tutorial pages) and click the card matching their goal, e.g. Regression. 5) In the Regression track they swap the synthetic dataset for their CSV path, read best_rmse/best_r2/result.top(), then `result.export('exports/best_model.n4a')` and `nirs4all.predict(model='exports/best_model.n4a', data=X_new)` — first model shipped. 6) Second task: from the bottom-of-track 'Next goals' links they jump straight to Preprocessing selection (compare SNV/MSC/Detrend via `_or_`, pick with result.top()) or Cross-validation — never returning to a front-door menu, always moving goal -> goal, with Concepts/Reference one click away whenever they want the why or the signature.
